text.title: Gcp Cloud Run Serverless Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for the gcp-cloud-run-serverless methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale + concrete remediation hook.

rule.statement: Cloud Run Services MUST be stateless — any persistence MUST live in Cloud SQL / Firestore / Cloud Storage.
rule.rationale: Instances are ephemeral and replaced freely; storing state locally produces inconsistent reads across requests.

rule.statement: Services MUST declare explicit `--concurrency` and `--cpu` values matching the application's request profile.
rule.rationale: Default concurrency (80) is wrong for CPU-heavy or single-threaded workloads; explicit values prevent silent throttling.

rule.statement: `--min-instances` value MUST be a documented decision (cost vs cold-start) — never left to platform default.
rule.rationale: Cold-start tolerance is product-driven; an undocumented default hides a financial trade-off.

rule.statement: Deployed images MUST reference Artifact Registry by digest (`@sha256:...`), not by floating tag.

rationale.source: Cloud Run + Artifact Registry security best-practice

rule.rationale: Digest pinning is the only way to make the runtime artefact reproducible and immutable.

rule.statement: Secrets MUST be mounted from Secret Manager (file or env), never embedded in image or deploy command.
rule.rationale: Image-embedded secrets leak into registries and logs; Secret Manager provides rotation + IAM + audit.

rule.statement: If the input fails ALL "Applies If" preconditions or matches ANY "Skip If" disqualifier, do not apply this methodology — route the task to the adjacent sibling instead.
rule.rationale: Even flat checklists need an explicit skip leaf in the decision tree; this rule is the universal terminal.
